love_and_relationships: Does the concept of a soulmate exist, or do we build love with someone we choose?
Is love a choice or a feeling? Can you choose to fall in love?
Is monogamy natural, or is it a social construct?
Can you love someone you don't trust? Can you trust someone you don't love?
Is it better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all?
Does true love require sacrifice, or should love never demand you lose yourself?
Is jealousy a sign of love or a sign of insecurity?
Can love exist without physical attraction? Can attraction exist without love?
Should you stay in a relationship for the sake of children?
Is it possible to love more than one person at the same time?
science_and_origins: Which came first, the chicken or the egg? Is there even an answer?
Is the universe infinite, or does it have an edge? What lies beyond?
Does time exist, or is it a human construct?
Is there life elsewhere in the universe? If we found it, what would it mean for humanity?
Is consciousness a product of the brain, or something beyond the physical?
Can science explain everything, or are there questions science cannot answer?
Is the human body designed, or did it evolve by chance?
What existed before the Big Bang? Is 'before' even a meaningful concept?
Is the universe deterministic, or do we have free will?
Can AI ever become conscious? If it does, does it deserve rights?
morality: Is morality universal or culturally relative?
Can a good person do bad things and still be considered good?
Where do our moral values come from?
If no one would ever find out, would it still be wrong to do something bad?
justice: Is the justice system truly fair to everyone?
Should punishment be about retribution or rehabilitation?
What is the purpose of prison?
Can true justice ever be achieved?
